Join the MSI

I. Postdoctoral Fellows

II. Technician and Research Fellows

I. POSTDOCTORAL FELLOWS

The Center for Genomic Experimentation & Computation (CGEC) seeks Research Fellows in the fields of biology, chemistry, physics, engineering, mathematics, and computer science to work on the Alpha Project, a multidisciplinary effort in genomics.

Supported by a grant awarded by the National Human Genome Research Institute (NHGRI), the Alpha Project brings together a team of investigators from various disciplines to work on an initiative that combines genomic experimentation with computer modeling. Fellows will examine communications within cells to develop computer models that accurately predict intra-cellular signaling. One of the ultimate goals of this research is to reveal insights that enable precisely targeted treatments for disease.

The core of CGEC is the Molecular Sciences Institute located in Berkeley, CA. Fellows may also work at specific laboratories in other institutions participating in CGEC: Massachusetts Institute of Technology; University of California, Berkeley; California Institute of Technology; and Pacific Northwest National Laboratory.

Applicants should submit, by May 1, 2003, a curriculum vitae, along with a cover letter explaining their background, goals, and what they expect to contribute to and learn from the Alpha Project. The applicant shall also request three letters of recommendation to be sent on his/her behalf.

Address the application to :
Molecular Sciences Institute
Attn: fellowships
2168 Shattuck Avenue
Floor 2
Berkeley, CA 94704
Phone 510-647-0690
Fax 510-647-0699
fellowships@molsci.org

II. TECHNICIAN AND RESEARCH FELLOW

Effective immediately, The Molecular Sciences Institute seeks a highly trained Technician and a Research Fellow to work on a newly developed means to detect and quantify small numbers of arbitrarily designated molecules. This work is under the aegis of the Alpha project at the MSI Center for Genomic Experimentation and Computation, and toward that end will be directed toward the quantification of different protein molecules in the mating pheromone response pathway in individual Saccharomyces cerevisiae cells. It is also likely that this work will prove useful in the detection and quantification of molecules useful in medical diagnosis, and in detection of molecules in the environment.

Technician. The technician will have a great deal of successful experience with recombinant DNA techniques and expressing and purifying proteins. Typically, the person will have 5 or more years successful post-undergraduate experience.

Research fellow. Will have a Ph.D. in the life sciences and will have demonstrated real accomplishment during their Ph.D. work evidenced by significant published papers. Candidate should have previous experience in nucleic acid manipulation and protein work, and will typically have experience in protein design and engineering. Fellow will be expected to lead in the design and development of new geometries and platforms for protein-based recognition molecules, protein aptamers and other protein molecules derived from or inspired antibodies, and to lead efforts to express and use these new molecules.

Applicants should send a curriculum vitae along with a cover letter explaining their background, their goals, and what they expect to be able to contribute to the above work. Technician applicants should include employment history and give names of three people who can recommend them. Research fellow applicants should give names of references and cause formal letters of recommendation to be sent on their behalf.

Applications should be submitted to:

Human Resources
The Molecular Sciences Institute
2168 Shattuck Avenue Floor 2
Berkeley, California 94704 USA

or by email: jobs@molsci.org

The Molecular Sciences Institute is an equal opportunity employer.
